entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species evaluated for resistance to rust and leaf blight.

entities.name: PUCCINIA PURPUREA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes rust in sorghum.

entities.name: RUST RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum accessions to resist infection by Puccinia purpurea.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The approach used to evaluate sorghum accessions for disease reaction in a natural environment.

entities.name: PATANCHERU, INDIA
entities.description: The geographical location where the field experiments were conducted.

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The specific seasonal timing of the initial sorghum evaluations.

entities.name: HIGH HUMIDITY
entities.description: Environmental condition maintained to facilitate pathogen infection during reassessment.

entities.name: SPRINKLER IRRIGATION
entities.description: The technique used to maintain high humidity in the field experiments.

entities.name: EXSEROHILUM TURCICUM
entities.description: The fungal pathogen causing leaf blight in sorghum.

entities.name: SETOSPHAERIA TURCICA
entities.description: A synonym for the fungal pathogen Exserohilum turcicum.

entities.name: LEAF BLIGHT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum accessions to resist infection by Exserohilum turcicum.

entities.name: PURPLE PIGMENTATION
entities.description: The presence of purple color in sorghum, typically associated with rust susceptibility.
